instruction: Walk straight until you can't anymore then turn left. Wait near the door way straight ahead.

text: Let me start by taking a closer look at what we're working with.
I see a wall panel or exhibit board ahead, so I should look around and turn to find the way forward.
I'm looking at a red-carpeted hallway and want to rotate my perspective to get a better sense of the full space around me.
I'm working through a full rotation to get a complete sense of the orientation.

I've rotated 270 degrees total, which brings me back to roughly the original direction. Now I'm looking down a corridor with red carpet stretching straight ahead and doors visible along the sides—this appears to be the main path to follow.

Let me continue turning to get a complete 360-degree view of the space.

I'm working through the spatial orientation by tracking the rotation angles. Starting from the original view facing the wall panel at 0 degrees, each 6-turn increment represents a 90-degree rotation. The red corridor appeared after 90 degrees of left turns, placing it to my right relative to the starting position. At 180 degrees I saw the painting and couch, and continuing this pattern at 270 degrees left (equivalent to 90 degrees right) I'm recalculating the spatial layout.

The instruction to walk straight until blocked, then turn left toward a doorway suggests the starting orientation should align with an open path, but the wall panel appears to block the direct forward view. I'm reconsidering whether the panel is a movable display rather than a solid wall, allowing passage around it, or whether the "straight" direction in the instructions actually corresponds to one of the other cardinal directions I've already mapped out. Let me try moving forward from the current position to see what happens.
I've hit a dead end going straight toward the panel, so the initial direction won't work. Now I'm turning left to explore the doorway that way.

I'm recognizing this red room with the red carpet from the earlier 90-degree left turn, and I'm noticing the dark doorway opening on the right side past the red curtain. I should continue turning left or move forward to get a better view of what's beyond that passage.
I notice there's a doorway visible on the right side past the red curtain, so I should turn slightly in that direction to face it directly.

I can see the doorway ahead with its brown frame positioned at the left-center, and the panel is back in view on the right side now that I've turned. Let me adjust my angle slightly to the left to get the doorway properly centered.
I can see the doorway ahead at center-right—a dark wooden frame next to the red curtain bed—so I'll head toward it.
I can see the doorway ahead on the right side, so I'm reorienting myself to face it directly and moving forward.
I notice there's a doorway visible in the center-left area, so I'll adjust the view slightly to the left to get it properly centered.
Doorway is now centered ahead. Let me move forward toward it.
I'm moving closer to the doorway now—I can see it clearly in the center with that dark opening and the green exit sign glowing above it.
I'm approaching the doorway now, keeping my movements deliberate as I close the distance.
The doorway is coming into view, positioned slightly to the right and within a few meters. I should move closer and adjust my position to center it, then hold steady.
The doorway is now clearly ahead and close. This looks like a good stopping point near the doorway as instructed.
I'm getting close to the doorway now, just a couple meters away. I should pause here and wait by the entrance ahead.
I am now close to the doorway as instructed. This is the goal location.

I have completed the navigation task. Summary of the path taken:
1. Started facing a museum display panel (dead end - couldn't walk straight further).
2. Turned left (~90°) into a red-walled room with a canopy bed and carpet.
3. Identified a doorway ahead (with exit sign) across the room.
4. Walked forward through the room, adjusting heading to keep the doorway centered.
5. Stopped within close range (~1-2m) of the doorway as instructed ("wait near the doorway straight ahead").

Episode ended successfully with STOP called at the target location.
